qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Workflow import-by-URL performs unrestricted server-side fetches
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the backend import-by-URL fetch path (`GET /workflows/from-url` -> `axios.get(query.url)`) and describes SSRF. The advisory is a stored XSS in markdown rendering used by sticky notes and other workflow UI surfaces, so the vulnerable code and root cause do not overlap.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's stored markdown-rendering XSS root cause. The only qualifying finding is a separate SSRF in the workflow import-by-URL backend fetch logic.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe stored cross-site scripting in a markdown rendering component used in n8n's interface, including workflow sticky notes and other markdown-capable UI areas.

The only final qualifying finding in the session manifest is `workflow_import_url_ssrf`, which points to `GET /workflows/from-url` and backend `axios.get(query.url)` behavior in `packages/cli/src/workflows/workflows.controller.ts`.

That finding does not involve markdown rendering, sticky note content, client-side script execution, or same-origin browser privileges, so it does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
